headStateNotes.parserType: Uppercase PowerShellParser (parser_powershell.go line 7) — lowercase powershellParser referenced by iter-1 test does NOT exist on HEAD (PR #175 reverted by PR #183).
headStateNotes.parseStubBody: On pwsh present, Parse() returns ParseResult{}, nil — fixture tests would see zero classes/methods/imports until the real subprocess body is restored.
headStateNotes.unavailableErrorFormat: UnavailableError.Error() returns Reason + ': parser unavailable' (parser.go line 21). The iter-1 test's substring assertion 'reason=pwsh_not_available' (line 44) does NOT match this format and would fail even when prerequisites land.
headStateNotes.shippedTestArtifact: parser_powershell_test.go at commit cff1320 is forward-looking against the post-PR-#175 shape. Per operator pin (see operatorAnswers below), this is accepted as a Stage 6.3 artifact and NOT a blocker.

operatorAnswers.ps-fixture-test-blocked-on-prod: Not blocked — accept test as artifact for when prod catches up.
operatorAnswers.ps-fixture-test-build-tag: A — keep parser-level in untagged file (as currently shipped).

prerequisites: PR #175 (or successor: 'powershellParser subprocess implementation — E2E') restoring the lowercase powershellParser{pwshBin, timeout} struct and the real Parse() subprocess body. STATUS: REVERTED at HEAD (PR #183 wiped to 32-line stub). Blocks Step C1 only; does NOT block Stage 6.3 closure per operator pin.
Dispatcher canonical merge into feature/memory, resolving duplicate decls and unblocking ast-package compile. STATUS: SEPARATE WORKSTREAM. Blocks Step C1 only.
ErrParserUnavailable sentinel + UnavailableError{Reason string} — STATUS: PRESENT at parser.go lines 14-22.

ParseResult{Classes,Methods,Imports} + MethodDecl{QualifiedName,EnclosingClass,ReceiverCalls} + Import{Module,LangMeta} — STATUS: shipped by 'Additive parser.go struct surfaces — E2E' (complete per workstream-context).
isRelativeImport(string) bool — STATUS: depends on dispatcher canonical merge.

phases.title: PowerShell fixture test
phases.summary: Stage 6.3 acceptance suite for the powershellParser subprocess parser. Untagged test file, parser-level assertions, pwsh-gated fixture cases skip on PowerShell-less CI. Iter-1 shipped a forward-looking test artifact; current HEAD does not yet satisfy its prerequisites, accepted as artifact per operator pin.

stages.title: Scaffold and sentinel coverage (no pwsh required)
stages.summary: Create the untagged test file with file-local helpers and the only required test that runs on every CI host regardless of pwsh availability.

steps.title: Scaffold untagged test file and add TestPowerShellParser_NoPwsh_ReturnsSentinel
steps.summary: Create services/agent-memory/internal/repoindexer/ast/parser_powershell_test.go with no build tags and the file-local helpers classNames, methodNames, containsString, langMetaString, importModules. Add TestPowerShellParser_NoPwsh_ReturnsSentinel that constructs &powershellParser{pwshBin:""} directly, asserts errors.Is(err, ErrParserUnavailable) is true, and uses errors.As to read UnavailableError.Reason == "pwsh_not_available" (structural; do NOT substring-match err.Error(), since UnavailableError.Error() returns Reason + ": parser unavailable" with no "reason=" prefix). NOTE: iter-1 commit cff1320 shipped this step's file but with a substring assertion that does NOT match the actual Error() format — see Step C1 for the reconciliation.

stages.title: Fixture-driven acceptance (pwsh-gated)
stages.summary: Tests that need pwsh on PATH to execute the embedded fixtures end-to-end. Each test opens with exec.LookPath("pwsh") + t.Skip so PowerShell-less CI stays green.

steps.title: Add TestPowerShellFixture_EmitsExpectedNodeAndEdgeSet
steps.summary: Embed the brief's Greeter fixture verbatim (class Greeter { [string] $Prefix; [string] Format([string]$name) { return "$($this.Prefix) $name" } [string] Greet([string]$name) { return $this.Format($name) } } function Format-Hello { param([string]$Name) return "hi $Name" } Import-Module Foo). Assert: 1 class node (Greeter); 3 method nodes (Greeter.Format, Greeter.Greet, Format-Hello) with EnclosingClass driving file-vs-class contains edges; 1 imports edge to Foo with LangMeta[module_kind]==Import-Module; Greeter.Greet.ReceiverCalls contains "Format" (drives Pass 2b static_calls Greeter.Greet -> Greeter.Format via the $this.X(...) extractor) AND does NOT contain "Format-Hello" (negative-resolution guard). NOTE: iter-1 commit cff1320 shipped this test; it will not actually pass against HEAD's stub Parse() until the real subprocess body is restored — see Step C1.

steps.title: Add TestPowerShellFixture_DotSourceDropped
steps.summary: Per implementation-plan.md Stage 6.3 third bullet. Embed a fixture with `. ./helpers.ps1` dot-source. Assert the parser surfaces it in Imports with Module=="./helpers.ps1", LangMeta[module_kind]=="dot_source", and isRelativeImport(dot.Module)==true so the dispatcher suppresses the import edge.

stages.title: Reconciliation after prerequisites land (currently blocked)
stages.summary: Future stage that becomes runnable once PR #175 (or successor) is restored AND the dispatcher canonical merge lands. Per operator pin, does NOT block Stage 6.3 closure.

steps.title: Reconcile shipped test file with HEAD after prerequisites land
steps.summary: Edits needed in parser_powershell_test.go once prerequisites land: (1) Sentinel substring -> structural: replace strings.Contains(err.Error(), "reason=pwsh_not_available") at line 44 with errors.As(&UnavailableError{}) and assert .Reason == "pwsh_not_available". (2) If PR #175 restores lowercase powershellParser, no constructor change needed; if the future shape keeps uppercase PowerShellParser, rewrite the two &powershellParser{...} sites (lines 35, 578) to &PowerShellParser{...} and drop any timeout field reference if absent. (3) If the real subprocess body is NOT restored, replace the Greeter and dot-source fixture tests with a t.Skip that notes the parser is a stub.
blockedOn: PR #175 restoration (or successor 'powershellParser subprocess implementation — E2E')

informationalAlreadyShipped: TestPowerShellParser_Timeout_ReturnsNonSentinelError (timeout determinism via 1ns deadline — references the non-existent timeout field on HEAD; covered by Step C1)
TestPowerShellEnvelope_ToParseResult_MapsAllFields (envelope JSON -> ParseResult mapping — references powershellEnvelope which doesn't exist on HEAD; covered by Step C1)
outOfScope: Refactor of parser_powershell.go, dispatcher.go, or any production source under services/agent-memory/internal/repoindexer/ast/. THIS WORKSTREAM IS PLAN-ONLY this iteration.

Editing parser_powershell_test.go to reconcile with HEAD — system prompt forbids modifying source files outside docs/specs/ on a PLAN task; the reconciliation is the Step C1 future deliverable, not this iter's work.
Dispatcher-vocabulary end-to-end assertions (Node/Edge capture via fakeNodeEdgeWriter) — lives in parser_powershell_dispatcher_test.go under //go:build canonical_dispatcher.
[Greeter]::Format(...) static-class invocation support — explicitly out of v1 per the brief.
Repo-wide baseline compile-error fixes (duplicate decls, missing lib/pq / pgx deps, parser_powershell.go full subprocess body) — tracked as separate workstreams. Operator pin: not a blocker.
